children.name: COVID-19
children.description: Coronavirus disease 2019 caused by SARS-CoV-2. A respiratory illness that can spread from person to person, ranging from mild to severe.

children.name: Influenza
children.description: Acute respiratory infection caused by influenza viruses. Seasonal epidemics occur globally with significant morbidity and mortality.

children.name: Dengue Fever
children.description: Mosquito-borne viral disease transmitted by Aedes mosquitoes. Can progress to dengue hemorrhagic fever or shock syndrome.

children.name: Malaria
children.description: Life-threatening disease caused by Plasmodium parasites transmitted through bites of infected Anopheles mosquitoes.

children.name: Tuberculosis
children.description: Chronic bacterial infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, primarily affecting the lungs. A major global health threat.

children.name: HIV/AIDS
children.description: Human immunodeficiency virus infection and acquired immunodeficiency syndrome. Attacks the immune system, specifically CD4 cells.

children.name: Cholera
children.description: Acute diarrhoeal infection caused by ingestion of Vibrio cholerae bacteria. Can be rapidly fatal without treatment.

children.name: Ebola Virus Disease
children.description: Severe, often fatal hemorrhagic fever in humans caused by Ebola virus. High case fatality rate.

children.name: Hepatitis B
children.description: Viral infection attacking the liver caused by Hepatitis B virus. Can become chronic and lead to cirrhosis or liver cancer.

children.name: Hepatitis C
children.description: Blood-borne viral infection caused by Hepatitis C virus. Major cause of chronic liver disease worldwide.

children.name: Measles
children.description: Highly contagious viral disease preventable by vaccination. Can cause severe complications including pneumonia and encephalitis.

children.name: Zika Virus Disease
children.description: Mosquito-borne viral disease primarily transmitted by Aedes mosquitoes. Associated with birth defects and neurological complications.

children.name: Yellow Fever
children.description: Acute viral hemorrhagic disease transmitted by infected mosquitoes. Preventable by vaccination.

children.name: Plague
children.description: Infectious disease caused by Yersinia pestis. Can manifest as bubonic, septicemic, or pneumonic plague.

children.name: Rabies
children.description: Fatal viral disease transmitted through bites of infected animals. Nearly 100% fatal once symptoms appear.

name: Chronic Non-Communicable Diseases
description: Endocrine, nutritional and metabolic diseases, and other chronic conditions

children.name: Type 2 Diabetes
children.description: Chronic metabolic disorder characterized by high blood sugar, insulin resistance, and relative lack of insulin.

children.name: Type 1 Diabetes
children.description: Autoimmune form of diabetes where the immune system destroys insulin-producing beta cells in the pancreas.

children.name: Hypertension
children.description: Persistently elevated arterial blood pressure. Major risk factor for heart disease, stroke, and kidney disease.

children.name: Coronary Artery Disease
children.description: Narrowing or blockage of coronary arteries, usually caused by atherosclerosis. Leading cause of death worldwide.

children.name: Stroke
children.description: Acute neurological injury due to disruption of blood supply to the brain. Second leading cause of death globally.

children.name: Breast Cancer
children.description: Malignant neoplasm of breast tissue. Most common cancer in women worldwide.

children.name: Lung Cancer
children.description: Malignant neoplasm of the lung. Leading cause of cancer death worldwide, strongly associated with smoking.

children.name: Asthma
children.description: Chronic inflammatory disease of the airways characterized by variable and recurring symptoms, reversible airflow obstruction.

children.name: COPD
children.description: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease — progressive lung disease causing obstructed airflow from the lungs.

children.name: Pneumonia
children.description: Inflammatory condition of the lung parenchyma primarily caused by infection. Leading infectious cause of death in children.

children.name: Alzheimer's Disease
children.description: Progressive neurodegenerative disease and the most common cause of dementia. Characterized by memory loss and cognitive decline.

children.name: Parkinson's Disease
children.description: Progressive nervous system disorder affecting movement. Develops gradually with tremor, stiffness, and slow movement.

children.name: Epilepsy
children.description: Chronic neurological disorder characterized by recurrent unprovoked seizures.

children.name: Major Depressive Disorder
children.description: Common and serious mood disorder causing persistent feelings of sadness and loss of interest.

children.name: Schizophrenia
children.description: Chronic severe mental disorder affecting thinking, feeling, and behavior. Characterized by psychotic symptoms.
